examples.title: Basic Structure Definition and Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates how to define a structure, declare variables, and access members
examples.explanation: This program defines a structure 'Student' with three members (rollno, name, marks), declares a variable s1, assigns values using the dot operator, and displays them.

examples.title: Structure Initialization
examples.description: Shows different ways to initialize structure variables at declaration
examples.explanation: Demonstrates three methods of structure initialization: complete initialization, partial initialization, and designated initialization (C99 feature allowing any order).

examples.title: Array of Structures
examples.description: Using array of structures to store multiple records
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates storing multiple student records using an array of structures. Each element of the array is a complete structure containing rollno, name, and marks.

examples.title: Structure with Pointer and Arrow Operator
examples.description: Demonstrates using pointers to structures and arrow operator for member access
examples.explanation: This program shows how to use a pointer to access structure members. The arrow operator (->) is a convenient shorthand for dereferencing and then using the dot operator.
